title: Listening - Part 1
description: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new meditation app.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: I’ve been using 'Zen-Mind' for a week and I actually feel much less frazzled by the evening. || Man: I tried it, but [1]I found the constant 'achievement badges' you earn a bit counterproductive[/1]. It felt like it was turning relaxation into a competitive sport. || Woman: I see what you mean. I just ignore that part. [2]What I really appreciate is the huge variety of ambient background sounds they offer[/2]. Most apps just have 'rain' or 'forest', but this has everything from 'busy library' to 'underwater'. || Man: True, the soundscapes are high quality. I just wish the subscription fee wasn't so steep for what it is. || Woman: It is expensive, but for me, the improved sleep quality makes it worth every penny.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’ve been using 'Zen-Mind' for a week and I actually feel much less frazzled by the evening.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I tried it, but I found the constant 'achievement badges' you earn a bit counterproductive. It felt like it was turning relaxation into a competitive sport.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I see what you mean. I just ignore that part. What I really appreciate is the huge variety of ambient background sounds they offer. Most apps just have 'rain' or 'forest', but this has everything from 'busy library' to 'underwater'.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: True, the soundscapes are high quality. I just wish the subscription fee wasn't so steep for what it is.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It is expensive, but for me, the improved sleep quality makes it worth every penny.

questions.question: What is the man's main criticism of the app?

options.C: the poor quality of the guided sessions

questions.explanation: He finds the 'achievement badges' counterproductive as they make it feel like a 'competitive sport' [1].

questions.question: The woman particularly likes the app because of

options.B: the effectiveness of the sleep tracking.

questions.explanation: She praises the 'huge variety of ambient background sounds' [2].

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about a change in the company's remote work policy.
extracts.audio_script: Man: So, the CEO wants us all back in the office three days a week starting next month. What's your take? || Woman: [3]I suspect the real reason is that they’re worried about the long-term lease on this building sitting empty[/3], rather than any actual drop in our productivity. || Man: That would make sense. It’s a lot of wasted rent. But I’m worried about the impact on our team's morale. || Woman: [4]I’ve noticed that our most junior staff are really struggling without the spontaneous learning that happens when we’re all together[/4]. You can't really replicate that on a video call. || Man: That's a fair point. It’s hard to mentor someone through a screen. || Woman: Exactly. It might be an unpopular move, but for the sake of the new starters, it’s probably necessary.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So, the CEO wants us all back in the office three days a week starting next month. What's your take?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suspect the real reason is that they’re worried about the long-term lease on this building sitting empty, rather than any actual drop in our productivity.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That would make sense. It’s a lot of wasted rent. But I’m worried about the impact on our team's morale.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’ve noticed that our most junior staff are really struggling without the spontaneous learning that happens when we’re all together. You can't really replicate that on a video call.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That's a fair point. It’s hard to mentor someone through a screen.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. It might be an unpopular move, but for the sake of the new starters, it’s probably necessary.

questions.question: Why does the woman believe the policy has been changed?

options.A: to address a decline in staff output
options.B: to justify the cost of the office space

questions.explanation: She suspects the reason is the 'long-term lease on this building sitting empty' [3].

questions.question: The woman suggests that returning to the office will specifically benefit

options.B: the mental health of the senior managers.
options.C: the professional development of newer employees.

questions.explanation: She notes that 'junior staff' benefit from 'spontaneous learning' that happens in person [4].

extracts.context: You hear two people discussing a new science-fiction film.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I finally saw 'Nebula Dawn' last night. The visual effects were staggering, especially the scenes on the ice planet. || Woman: They were impressive. But [5]I found the plot a bit too derivative of earlier classics in the genre[/5]. It felt like I’d seen it all before in 'Interstellar' or 'Blade Runner'. || Man: I see what you mean. It didn't break much new ground narratively. || Woman: [6]I just wish the lead actor had been given a bit more emotional depth to work with[/6]. His performance felt a bit one-dimensional, even during the climactic scenes. || Man: I agree. It felt like they spent ninety percent of the budget on the CGI and forgot to hire a decent scriptwriter. || Woman: Exactly. It’s a beautiful film to look at, but it didn't leave much of a lasting impression.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I finally saw 'Nebula Dawn' last night. The visual effects were staggering, especially the scenes on the ice planet.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: They were impressive. But I found the plot a bit too derivative of earlier classics in the genre. It felt like I’d seen it all before in 'Interstellar' or 'Blade Runner'.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I see what you mean. It didn't break much new ground narratively.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I just wish the lead actor had been given a bit more emotional depth to work with. His performance felt a bit one-dimensional, even during the climactic scenes.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It felt like they spent ninety percent of the budget on the CGI and forgot to hire a decent scriptwriter.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s a beautiful film to look at, but it didn't leave much of a lasting impression.

questions.question: What was the woman's main issue with the film?

options.A: the slow pacing of the story
options.B: the lack of originality in the script
options.C: the poor quality of the visual effects

questions.explanation: She found the plot 'too derivative' of earlier classics [5].

questions.question: Both speakers agree that the film

options.A: failed to develop its characters effectively.
options.B: was too long for the amount of content.
options.C: should have used less computer-generated imagery.

questions.explanation: They agree the lead actor's performance was 'one-dimensional' and they 'forgot to hire a decent scriptwriter' [6].
